figures.caption: Figure 1: Implementation of SC-SDPO. The entire modification reduces to computing a per-question scalar weight from on-policy rollouts (Steps 1–3) and a single element-wise multiplication on the existing SDPO loss (Step 4). No architectural changes, additional rollouts, or hyperparameter tuning beyond the default α = 0.5 are required.
Table 1: Qwen3-8B results comparing mean@16 and maj@16 (%). Bold and underlined values indicate the best and second-best results, respectively.

Figure 4: KL-based analysis of the base model (Qwen3-8B). Top row: kernel density estimates of each metric, stratified by whether the individual rollout is correct (blue) or incorrect (red). Bottom row: per-metric mean ± one standard deviation as a function of the question-level pass rate (fraction of correct rollouts among 16, ranging from 0.0625 to 1).
Figure 2: Average response length (in tokens) over 200 training steps, averaged across five datasets.

Figure 3: Averaged training dynamics across five datasets. (Left) Mean pass rate per training step. (Middle) Fraction of training samples in pass-rate bins 1 8– 7 8 per 50-step window. (Right) Actor gradient norm per training step.
